2024-02-01.title: A federal judge dismisses a suit to block U.S. support of Israel — but urges Biden to re-examine his approach.

2024-02-01.abstract: A scandal involving the president’s spouse has become a major issue for his party ahead of a crucial parliamentary election.

2024-02-03.abstract: A proposal that would weaken term limits at European soccer’s governing body has insiders and governance officials worried that sports’ power brokers are forgetting why the rules exist.

2024-02-03.abstract: A celebration of (at least occasional) shoelessness.

2024-02-05.abstract: Footage from China Central Television, a state media outlet, showed vehicles moving slowly on highways blanketed by snow as the Lunar New Year travel rush begins.

2024-02-05.abstract: The verdict in the case of Yang Hengjun, who was detained on national security charges, may weigh on the warming relations between China and Australia.

Blinken met with leaders in Egypt and Qatar on Tuesday, the second day of a Middle East tour aimed at preventing an exchange of attacks with Iran-backed militias from spiraling into a broader regional war and to rally allies around a proposed cease-fire agreement for Gaza.

2024-02-24.title: Russia’s Brutal War Calculus
2024-02-24.abstract: The costs of two years of war in Ukraine have been enormous. But many Russians are feeling optimistic.

2024-02-24.title: Why the Cost of Success in English Soccer’s Lower Leagues Keeps Going Up
2024-02-24.abstract: Buying a small-town club offers a tempting entry to ownership. But the sport’s economics mean even multimillionaires can struggle to compete.

2024-02-24.title: Sanitation Crisis in Gaza Spreads Disease
2024-02-24.abstract: “It is a public health concern,” one U.N. official said of the lack of toilets for displaced people in the territory. “But the second thing is simply just dignity.”

2024-02-24.title: For Car Thieves, Toronto Is a ‘Candy Store,’ and Drivers Are Fed Up
2024-02-24.abstract: An epidemic of auto thefts in Canada’s largest city has left many residents exasperated, with some getting creative about deterrence efforts, such as installing bollards in home driveways.

2024-02-25.title: The Spy War: How the C.I.A. Secretly Helps Ukraine Fight Putin
2024-02-25.abstract: For more than a decade, the United States has nurtured a secret intelligence partnership with Ukraine that is now critical for both countries in countering Russia.

2024-02-25.title: In Latin America, a New Frontier for Women: Professional Softball in Mexico
2024-02-25.abstract: For the first time, women professional softball players in Latin America have a league of their own, another gain for women seeking more opportunities.

2024-02-25.title: A Re-established West Bank Settlement Symbolizes Hardened Israeli Views
2024-02-25.abstract: Homesh, one of the four West Bank settlements dismantled by Israel when it withdrew from Gaza in 2005, has taken on new importance since Oct. 7 and the war against Hamas.

2024-02-25.title: Weary but Determined, Ukrainians Vow Never to Bow to Russia
2024-02-25.abstract: Despite death, destruction and deprivation, nearly 90 percent still believe in Ukraine’s ultimate victory — as long as Western aid continues.

2024-02-25.title: In Russia, Knowing That Her Son Is Dead, and Waiting for Him Anyway
2024-02-25.abstract: She watched, distressed, as Aleksei was sent off to war. Now, 14 months after he died in eastern Ukraine, Yulia Seleznyova holds a sliver of hope for a miracle.

2024-02-26.title: Israeli Forces Will Move Into Rafah, Cease-Fire Deal or Not, Netanyahu Says
2024-02-26.abstract: Amid signs of progress toward a pause in the fighting, Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Israeli forces planned to keep pushing into Gaza.

2024-02-26.title: Senate Aide Investigated Over Unofficial Actions in Ukraine
2024-02-26.abstract: Kyle Parker says he delivered sniper gear as part of his unabashed support for Ukraine. Investigators say there may be “counterintelligence issues.”

2024-02-26.title: Top U.N. Court Holds Last Day of Hearings on Israeli Occupation
2024-02-26.abstract: The hearings on the legality of Israel’s occupation of Palestinian territories have put more pressure on Israel during the war in Gaza.

2024-02-26.title: Macron Does Not Rule Out Sending Western Troops to Ukraine
2024-02-26.abstract: The French leader said that talks had not resulted in any consensus among European officials on putting troops on the ground in an “official” way, but that “anything is possible.”

2024-02-26.title: Israel Launches Its Deepest Airstrikes in Lebanon in Years
2024-02-26.abstract: At least two Hezbollah fighters were killed in strikes in the Bekaa Valley, and at least six other people were wounded, the mayor of a nearby city said.

2024-02-27.title: Israelis Broach a Concession in Hostage Talks With Hamas
2024-02-27.abstract: Israel negotiators are reported to be considering the release of high-profile Palestinians convicted of terrorism in exchange for freedom for some of the hostages in Gaza.

2024-02-27.title: After U.S. Strikes, Iran’s Proxies Scale Back Attacks on American Bases
2024-02-27.abstract: Tehran, wary of igniting open warfare with Washington, has told militia groups it backs to curtail assaults on targets such as military installations, Iranian and American officials say.

2024-02-27.title: Lead-Tainted Applesauce Sailed Through Gaps in Food-Safety System
2024-02-27.abstract: Hundreds of American children were poisoned last year. Records show how, time and again, the contamination went unnoticed.

2024-02-27.title: Pankaj Udhas, Bollywood Singer and Maestro of the Ghazal, Dies at 72
2024-02-27.abstract: His soulful renditions of ghazals, or traditional love poems, were featured on the soundtracks of hit Bollywood movies and moved generations of Indians.

2024-02-27.title: In Ukraine, Russia Is Inching Forward Death by Death
2024-02-27.abstract: Russian forces have an unorthodox view of acceptable levels of military losses, with a willingness to expend troops and equipment to make even small gains on the battlefield.

2024-02-28.title: Hamas Rejects Cease-Fire Proposal, Dashing Biden’s Hopes of Near Term Deal
2024-02-28.abstract: A day after President Biden suggested there could be a deal as soon as Monday, a Hamas official indicated the group would not trade Israeli troops held hostage for Palestinians imprisoned for terrorism.

2024-02-28.title: Back From War, Reserve Soldiers Set Their Sights on Israel’s Politics as Usual
2024-02-28.abstract: Inspired by the unity they experienced in the military, thousands of reservists returning from the front line in Gaza are organizing for political change.

2024-02-28.title: China Expands Scope of ‘State Secrets’ Law in Security Push
2024-02-28.abstract: The legal change, which could further limit access to information, is part of an increasingly hostile environment facing foreign businesses in the country.

2024-02-28.title: Watchdog Finds E.U. Border Agency Too Weak to Prevent Migrant Disasters at Sea
2024-02-28.abstract: Investigators looking into the drowning of more than 600 people off Greece say that the border agency known as Frontex cannot fulfill its mission.

2024-02-28.title: A Land Once Emptied by War Now Faces a Peacetime Exodus
2024-02-28.abstract: Bosnia is being hit by a combination of a low birthrate and emigration, a trend fueled by ethnic tensions and disgust with corruption.

2024-02-29.title: In Britain, Shockwaves From Israel-Hamas War Are Jolting Domestic Politics
2024-02-29.abstract: The United States and several European nations have been caught up in toxic debates that have altered election campaigns and led to eruptions of inflammatory language over the conflict.

2024-02-29.title: What to Know About the Doctor Protests in South Korea
2024-02-29.abstract: More than a week after thousands of doctors walked off the job, their dispute with the government shows no signs of resolution.

2024-02-29.title: Gas Pumps Didn’t Work in New Zealand. Blame the Leap Year.
2024-02-29.abstract: Dozens of unattended fuel stations across the country stopped working on Thursday for hours because of a software issue.

2024-02-29.title: The ‘Luxury Route’ to the U.S. for African Migrants
2024-02-29.abstract: Colombia’s main airport has become a hub for migrants heading to the U.S. in greater numbers. Some have been stranded for weeks, or forcibly turned back.

2024-02-29.title: Welcome to Japan, Where the Bad News Is the Good News
2024-02-29.abstract: The trend lines may be grim, but the Japanese aren’t sweating them.
